comment_text.sn36.10:1.4: That is to say, certain kinds of contact are prone to create pleasant feelings. MN 140:19.4 treats these contacts in relation to the feelings experienced in absorptions.

root_text.sn36.10:1.1: “Tisso imā, bhikkhave, vedanā phassajā phassamūlakā phassanidānā phassapaccayā.
root_text.sn36.10:1.3: Sukhā vedanā, dukkhā vedanā, adukkhamasukhā vedanā.
root_text.sn36.10:1.4: Sukhavedaniyaṁ, bhikkhave, phassaṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhā vedanā.
root_text.sn36.10:1.5: Tasseva sukhavedaniyassa phassassa nirodhā, yaṁ tajjaṁ vedayitaṁ sukhavedaniyaṁ phassaṁ paṭicca uppannā sukhā vedanā, sā nirujjhati, sā vūpasammati.
root_text.sn36.10:1.6: Dukkhavedaniyaṁ, bhikkhave, phassaṁ paṭicca uppajjati dukkhā vedanā.
root_text.sn36.10:1.7: Tasseva dukkhavedaniyassa phassassa nirodhā, yaṁ tajjaṁ vedayitaṁ dukkhavedaniyaṁ phassaṁ paṭicca uppannā dukkhā vedanā, sā nirujjhati, sā vūpasammati.
root_text.sn36.10:1.8: Adukkhamasukhavedaniyaṁ, bhikkhave, phassaṁ paṭicca uppajjati adukkhamasukhā vedanā.
root_text.sn36.10:1.9: Tasseva adukkhamasukhavedaniyassa phassassa nirodhā, yaṁ tajjaṁ vedayitaṁ adukkhamasukhavedaniyaṁ phassaṁ paṭicca uppannā adukkhamasukhā vedanā, sā nirujjhati, sā vūpasammati.
root_text.sn36.10:1.10: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, dvinnaṁ kaṭṭhānaṁ saṅghaṭṭanasamodhānā usmā jāyati, tejo abhinibbattati. Tesaṁyeva kaṭṭhānaṁ nānābhāvā vinikkhepā, yā tajjā usmā, sā nirujjhati, sā vūpasammati.
root_text.sn36.10:1.11: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, imā tisso vedanā phassajā phassamūlakā phassanidānā phassapaccayā.
root_text.sn36.10:1.12: Tajjaṁ phassaṁ paṭicca tajjā vedanā uppajjanti.
root_text.sn36.10:1.13: Tajjassa phassassa nirodhā tajjā vedanā nirujjhantī”ti.

translation_text.sn36.10:1.1: “Mendicants, these three feelings are born, rooted, sourced, and dependent on contact.
translation_text.sn36.10:1.4: Pleasant feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as pleasant.
translation_text.sn36.10:1.5: With the cessation of that contact to be experienced as pleasant, the corresponding pleasant feeling ceases and stops.
translation_text.sn36.10:1.6: Painful feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as painful.
translation_text.sn36.10:1.7: With the cessation of that contact to be experienced as painful, the corresponding painful feeling ceases and stops.
translation_text.sn36.10:1.8: Neutral feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as neutral.
translation_text.sn36.10:1.9: With the cessation of that contact to be experienced as neutral, the corresponding neutral feeling ceases and stops.
translation_text.sn36.10:1.10: When you rub two sticks together, heat is generated and fire is produced. But when you part the sticks and lay them aside, any corresponding heat ceases and stops.
translation_text.sn36.10:1.11: In the same way, these three feelings are born, rooted, sourced, and dependent on contact.
translation_text.sn36.10:1.12: The appropriate feeling arises dependent on the corresponding contact.
translation_text.sn36.10:1.13: When the corresponding contact ceases, the appropriate feeling ceases.”

variant_text.sn36.10:1.10: saṅghaṭṭanasamodhānā → saṅkhattā tassa samodānā (cck, sya1ed, km); saṅghaṭṭā tassa samodhānā (sya2ed); saṅghattā tassa samodhānā (mr)
